<!-- Generated by box-gantry. DO NOT EDIT. -->
# Authentication

Construct a `dev.unofficialbox.Client` from one of the four Box auth flows, built
via `dev.unofficialbox.auth.Auth`. Each returns a
`dev.unofficialbox.runtime.Runtime.Auth` token source that caches its access token
until shortly before it expires; the network layer refreshes on a `401` and
retries once. (Config types below stay on `Runtime`, e.g. `Runtime.CcgConfig`.)

## Developer Token

```java
Client client = new Client(Auth.developerToken("DEVELOPER_TOKEN"));
```

## Client Credentials Grant (server auth, no key)

```java
Client client = new Client(Auth.clientCredentials(
Runtime.CcgConfig.enterprise("CLIENT_ID", "CLIENT_SECRET", "ENTERPRISE_ID")));
// or Runtime.CcgConfig.user("CLIENT_ID", "CLIENT_SECRET", "USER_ID")
```

## JWT (server auth with a signing key)

```java
// From an app's box_config.json (bad key: fails loudly at construction):
Client client = new Client(Auth.jwt(Runtime.JwtConfig.fromBoxConfig(boxConfigJson)));
// or build it explicitly:
Runtime.JwtConfig config = Runtime.JwtConfig.enterprise(
"CLIENT_ID", "CLIENT_SECRET", "PUBLIC_KEY_ID",
privateKeyPem, keyPassphrase, "ENTERPRISE_ID");
Client client = new Client(Auth.jwt(config));
```

## OAuth 2.0 (authorization code)

Redirect the user to the consent URL, exchange the returned code, then reuse the
stored (rotated) refresh token on later runs:

```java
Runtime.OAuthConfig oauth = new Runtime.OAuthConfig("CLIENT_ID", "CLIENT_SECRET");
// first run: redirect to oauth.authorizeUrl(redirectUri, state), then
// Auth auth = oauth.exchangeCode(code, redirectUri);
// later runs: rebuild from the stored refresh token.
Client client = new Client(Auth.oauth(oauth, storedRefreshToken));
```
